mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance, leading to expectations of higher future policy rates and consequently driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield consistently declines over 20-day periods when CPIAUCSL is above 280, or if the correlation between CPIAUCSL and yield direction is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate below 4% signals a tight labor market, contributing to wage inflation pressures and reinforcing expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which pushes the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% (t-1mo) indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows no significant upward trend over 20-day periods when UNRATE is below 4.0% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, the Federal Reserve is compelled to raise the FEDFUNDS rate aggressively. This strong signal of monetary tightening drives up the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as market participants price in higher future rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 287.708 and UNRATE at 3.6% are both at extreme levels
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield declines or remains flat over 60-day periods when both CPIAUCSL is above 280 and UNRATE is below 4.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) and strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate resilient consumer demand and economic growth. In an environment of rising inflation, this strength suggests the economy can absorb higher interest rates, pushing the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 665726.0 and HOUST at 1769.0 are strong
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield consistently falls over 20-day periods despite strong RSAFS and HOUST readings when CPIAUCSL is also elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy and expanding supply side of the economy. This growth, especially when coupled with strong demand, can lead to higher long-term growth expectations and thus higher equilibrium real interest rates, driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows no positive reaction over 5-day periods when INDPRO is consistently above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150 million indicates a robust labor market and strong economic activity. This strength reinforces expectations of continued economic expansion and potential inflationary pressures, leading to higher 10-year U.S. Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield consistently declines over 20-day periods when PAYEMS is above 150 million for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Even with a low FEDFUNDS rate, if inflation (CPIAUCSL) is significantly elevated, the market anticipates aggressive future rate hikes. This forward-looking expectation of monetary tightening, despite the current low policy rate, drives the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield consistently falls or remains flat over 60-day periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL is above 280.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a healthy economy with strong underlying demand. This fundamental strength supports higher long-term interest rates as investors expect continued economic expansion and potentially higher inflation, driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield consistently declines over 20-day periods when GDPC1 shows positive year-over-year growth.
